terms.label: Self-directed discovery
terms.definition: The player chooses to investigate and experiences the conclusion as personally discovered.

terms.label: Rule mastery
terms.definition: The player forms, tests, revises, and finally combines a reliable model of world rules.

terms.label: Spatial memory
terms.definition: The player builds a useful mental map through landmarks, loops, shortcuts, and revisits.

terms.label: World recontextualization
terms.definition: New knowledge, capability, or state gives earlier locations and objects a different meaning.

terms.label: Consequential agency
terms.definition: Player action visibly changes routes, characters, information, or future choices in the world.

terms.label: Embodied revelation
terms.definition: A story fact is understood through action, space, or performance before explanatory dialogue.

terms.label: Curiosity and tension
terms.definition: Visible anomalies and incomplete information sustain voluntary investigation without opaque withholding.

terms.label: Competence growth
terms.definition: Increasingly expressive use of a stable verb set makes the player feel more capable over time.

terms.label: Social recognition
terms.definition: NPC behavior and shared spaces acknowledge what the player has learned or changed.

terms.label: Fair recovery
terms.definition: Failure communicates cause, preserves learning, and returns the player to meaningful action quickly.

governance.changePolicy: Experience goals are extended only with cross-fixture evidence and a backward-compatible migration.
